text.title: backup-kubernetes-velero Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for backup-kubernetes-velero as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Agent emits `--default-volumes-to-restic` or `--use-restic` for Velero v1.15+ installations.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Restic path is deprecated in Velero v1.15. Use --use-node-agent and --default-volumes-to-fs-backup with Kopia. Anchor the prompt to the exact installed Velero version to avoid this.

failure-mode.description: Agent generates Velero Schedule with cron expression "0 2 * * *" without noting the timezone, causing the schedule to run at 2 AM UTC regardless of the team's local time — potentially during peak traffic hours for non-UTC teams.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Velero schedules run in UTC by default. "0 2 * * *" stomps on traffic peaks for teams in UTC+3 to UTC+8 time zones. Add a comment documenting the UTC schedule and its local equivalent for the ops team.

failure-mode.description: Agent generates Velero Backup that includes kube-system, velero, and other platform namespaces in the includedNamespaces wildcard without explicitly excluding them.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Backing up kube-system and velero namespaces adds noise, inflates backup size, and can cause restore conflicts. Always exclude them with excludedNamespaces in wildcard schedules.

failure-mode.description: Agent treats Velero CSI snapshot as equivalent to an off-cluster backup — "snapshot = backup".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: CSI snapshots live on the same storage system. If the storage system fails (cloud provider outage, cluster deletion), all snapshots go with it. Always configure a BackupStorageLocation in a separate account/region that copies backup data off the cluster's storage.
